```text
ARCHITECT DECISIONS:
- Production direction = faithful event-loop owner (option A): hotspot evidence shows __psynch_mutexwait at 22,512 samples on GET p100 and 18,728 on INCR p100 vs 118/227 total rust_user samples; dispatch/parse/alloc cost is now noise next to the per-command Arc<Mutex<RedisDb>>.
- Sharding out of scope for this milestone: MULTI/WATCH/SELECT/blocking/scripts/replication ordering all assume single-threaded effects.
- Single ordered RuntimeEvent enum, single channel: AOF/replication/pub/sub/blocked-keys interleaving require one totally-ordered event stream into the owner.
- Inert scaffold rule for runtime-owner-2-scaffold-types: no new workspace dep, no concrete poller, no default product-path change.
- TLS stays on the existing per-thread path this milestone; tls_migration_sequencing is human-blocking.
- Public claim is alpha telemetry only; no speed-parity language from a non-default mode.
- Authoritative subsystem ownership table (accept / socket-read / parse / dispatch / db / socket-write / cron / active-expire / pub-sub / blocked / aof / replication / rdb / tls) recorded with upstream Valkey C references; translator packets must work to that table.
- Four explicit TODO(human) gates added (poller dep, TLS sequencing, I/O thread parity, soak runner); no runtime-owner-3+ packet dispatches until they are answered.

FILES UPDATED:
- harness/architecture/decisions/runtime-ownership.md: rewritten with evidence table, subsystem boundary, locked decisions, TODO(human) gates, stop conditions.
- harness/architecture/lifecycle-map.toml: added evidence fields, decisions table, open_questions table, and [[subsystem]] array with upstream Valkey references and target owners; marked owner-loop transition blocked-on-human.
- harness/architecture/object-vocabulary.tsv: added ClientWriteBuffer, OwnerCommandResult, PollDriverHandle; clarified RuntimeOwner/ClientSlot/RuntimeEvent/RuntimeOwnerConfig/CommandContext/RedisDb notes; enumerated RuntimeEvent variants as informational rows.
- harness/work-packets.jsonl: hardened runtime-owner-2-scaffold-types note to explicitly forbid new workspace deps (mio/polling/tokio/libc-epoll), require PollDriverHandle to stay abstract, and cross-reference the object vocabulary.
- docs/RUNTIME_OWNERSHIP_PLAN.md: prepended "Refinement 2026-05-21" section pointing at the typed artifacts as the binding source; original architecture write-up preserved.

PACKETS CREATED OR CHANGED:
- runtime-owner-2-scaffold-types (translator): note tightened — same dependencies (runtime-owner-post-canary-oracle), same targets, additional explicit prohibitions on new deps and concrete poller imports.
- No new packets added. Existing graph (runtime-owner-0 -> 1-canary -> post-canary-oracle -> 2-scaffold -> post-scaffold-oracle -> post-scaffold-profile-matrix -> post-scaffold-hotspots) is sufficient for the unattended run; real owner-loop packets are deliberately not authored until the TODO(human) gates are answered.

HUMAN QUESTIONS:
- Poller dependency for runtime-owner-3 (real owner loop): mio (recommended) vs polling vs tokio vs raw libc. Approval required to add to crates/redis-server/Cargo.toml.
- TLS migration sequencing: never in this port, follow-up after owner default, or pre-default? Gates whether crates/redis-core/src/connection.rs may change in owner-loop packets.
- I/O thread parity with upstream's trySendPollJobToIOThreads: skip, follow-up packet, or match upstream now? Default recommendation: follow-up.
- Soak runner: harness/bench/run-soak.sh does not exist; before any public performance claim, an explicit runtime-owner-soak runner must land.

NEXT SAFE DISPATCH:
- runtime-owner-1-canary-corpus (translator): now unblocked. Its note already constrains it to oracle corpus + canary doc; the binding subsystem boundary in the refined decision doc tells the translator which semantics must be stressed (transaction order, WATCH invalidation, expiration-on-access, pub/sub non-interference, blocking-command edges, SELECT, script/replication-safe ordering).
